html:
The Tent is a Survival structure that can be used to sleep through dusk and night, like an immobile Straw Roll or Fur Roll. It will restore 50 Sanity and 60 Health at the cost of 75 Hunger, and set the player's body temperature to maximum when used. It requires 6 Silk, 4 Twigs, 3 Rope to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. The player is also immune to any damage except fire while using the tent.

Tents can be used six times before disappearing. If the player destroys one with a Hammer, the number of resources returned is inversely proportional to number of times the player's used the Tent (the more times the Tent is used, the fewer resources it will drop when demolished).

Tents are unusable by Wickerbottom due to her insomnia.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants and Shipwrecked DLCs, the behavior changes so that if the sleeper's temperature is below 35, then it will be set to 35. It may be more useful in Winter, where freezing is the prominent threat and dusk and night are much longer. Sleeping also reduces Wetness by 30.

Tips

  • Unequipping any items that lose durability while worn (e.g. Winter Hat, Miner Hat) before sleeping will save their durability.
  • In the base game, you can save some Hunger by using the Tent when your Hunger is lower than 75. If it is lower than 26 however, the character will refuse to go to sleep.
  • When used in tandem with a Siesta Lean-to (RoG must be enabled), the Tent can allow a player to quickly skip days or even seasons at the expense of food. Alternatively, Cursed Woodie can accomplish a similar feat by repeatedly transforming into werebeaver and letting the log meter deplete fast.
  • Even though Wormwood cannot heal from eating food, the Tent allows him to deal with this restriction by converting Hunger points to Health (and Sanity). Assuming there is an abundant supply of food, Wormwood can repeatedly replenish his health this way in order to farm valuable Living Logs.
  • Wearing a Slurper, Belt of Hunger, Hibearnation Vest or Funcap or playing as Wolfgang, Wes, or Warly doesn't change the Hunger cost of sleeping in a Tent.
  • The Tent is one of the few structures in the game that costs Silk. Having it prebuilt can provide some emergency silk when carrying a Hammer to save inventory space.

 Trivia

  • The Tent's original use was to allow the player to change their character. Crafting the tent cost more resources (15 Silk, 6 Twigs, and 3 Ropes), its crafting description was "Change your skin", and the Tent would be destroyed after one use. This functionality was changed in the 2013 Progress! update.

Bugs

  • Sometimes after a 100% torch is equipped before sleeping, after the character sleeps the torch will still have a durability of 20%, and it will not lose durability. Unequipping and equipping the torch again will fix the bug.
  • Sleeping while taking damage from Freezing may cause the edges of the screen to be stuck red. This will persist until the game is reloaded or if the character starts taking freezing damage again and warms back up.
  • Sometimes a tent may be used 7 times before disappearing.

Assets

  • idle
  • destroyed
  • place
  • sleeping

Gallery

  • A burnt Tent from the Reign of Giants DLC.
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • A Tent as it appears in The Screecher.
  • Three Tents made whilst playing as Wickerbottom.
Craftable ItemsStructures

The Tent is a Survival structure that can be used to sleep through dusk and night, like an immobile Straw Roll or Fur Roll. It will restore 50 Sanity and 60 Health at the cost of 75 Hunger, and set the player's body temperature to maximum when used. It requires 6 Silk, 4 Twigs, 3 Rope to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. The player is also immune to any damage except fire while using the tent.
Tents can be used six times before disappearing. If the player destroys one with a Hammer, the number of resources returned is inversely proportional to number of times the player's used the Tent (the more times the Tent is used, the fewer resources it will drop when demolished).
Tents are unusable by Wickerbottom due to her insomnia.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Reign of Giants and Shipwrecked DLCs, the behavior changes so that if the sleeper's temperature is below 35, then it will be set to 35. It may be more useful in Winter, where freezing is the prominent threat and dusk and night are much longer. Sleeping also reduces Wetness by 30.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Unequipping any items that lose durability while worn (e.g. Winter Hat, Miner Hat) before sleeping will save their durability.
In the base game, you can save some Hunger by using the Tent when your Hunger is lower than 75. If it is lower than 26 however, the character will refuse to go to sleep.
When used in tandem with a Siesta Lean-to (RoG must be enabled), the Tent can allow a player to quickly skip days or even seasons at the expense of food. Alternatively, Cursed Woodie can accomplish a similar feat by repeatedly transforming into werebeaver and letting the log meter deplete fast.
Even though Wormwood cannot heal from eating food, the Tent allows him to deal with this restriction by converting Hunger points to Health (and Sanity). Assuming there is an abundant supply of food, Wormwood can repeatedly replenish his health this way in order to farm valuable Living Logs.
Wearing a Slurper, Belt of Hunger, Hibearnation Vest or Funcap or playing as Wolfgang, Wes, or Warly doesn't change the Hunger cost of sleeping in a Tent.
The Tent is one of the few structures in the game that costs Silk. Having it prebuilt can provide some emergency silk when carrying a Hammer to save inventory space.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Tent's original use was to allow the player to change their character. Crafting the tent cost more resources (15 Silk, 6 Twigs, and 3 Ropes), its crafting description was "Change your skin", and the Tent would be destroyed after one use. This functionality was changed in the 2013 Progress! update.
Bugs[edit | edit source]
Sometimes after a 100% torch is equipped before sleeping, after the character sleeps the torch will still have a durability of 20%, and it will not lose durability. Unequipping and equipping the torch again will fix the bug.
Sleeping while taking damage from Freezing may cause the edges of the screen to be stuck red. This will persist until the game is reloaded or if the character starts taking freezing damage again and warms back up.
Sometimes a tent may be used 7 times before disappearing.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Lều là một cấu trúc Sinh tồn có thể được sử dụng để ngủ qua hoàng hôn và ban đêm , giống như Cuộn Rơm hoặc Cuộn Lông bất động . Nó sẽ khôi phục 50 Tinh Thần và 60 Máu với cái giá là 75 Sức Đói, đồng thời đặt nhiệt độ cơ thể của người chơi ở mức tối đa khi sử dụng. Nó cần 6 Tơ, 4 Cành Cây, 3 Dây Thừng để chế tạo và Máy Luyện Kim để nguyên mẫu. Người chơi cũng miễn nhiễm với mọi sát thương ngoại trừ lửa khi sử dụng lều.

The '''Tent''' is a [[Survival]] [[structure]] that can be used to sleep through [[Day-Night Cycle|dusk and night]], like an immobile [[Straw Roll]] or [[Fur Roll]]. It will restore 50 [[Sanity]] and 60 [[Health]] at the cost of 75 [[Hunger]], and set the player's body temperature to maximum when used. It requires 6 [[Silk]], 4 [[Twigs]], 3 [[Rope]] to craft and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. The player is also immune to any damage except fire while using the tent.

Tents can be used six times before disappearing. If the player destroys one with a [[Hammer]], the number of resources returned is inversely proportional to number of times the player's used the Tent (the more times the Tent is used, the fewer resources it will drop when demolished).

Tents are unusable by [[Wickerbottom]] due to her insomnia.

==[[File:Gift Icon.png|32px|link=]] Downloadable Content==
In the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' and ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLCs, the behavior changes so that if the sleeper's temperature is below 35, then it will be set to 35. It may be more useful in [[Winter]], where freezing is the prominent threat and dusk and night are much longer. Sleeping also reduces [[Wetness]] by 30.

==[[File:Prototype.png|32px|link=]] Tips==
* Unequipping any items that lose [[durability]] while worn (e.g. [[Winter Hat]], [[Miner Hat]]) before sleeping will save their durability.
* In the base game, you can save some Hunger by using the Tent when your Hunger is lower than 75. If it is lower than 26 however, the character will refuse to go to sleep.
* When used in tandem with a [[Siesta Lean-to]] ([[RoG]] must be enabled), the Tent can allow a player to quickly skip days or even seasons at the expense of food. Alternatively, [[Cursed Woodie]] can accomplish a similar feat by repeatedly transforming into werebeaver and letting the log meter deplete fast.
*Even though [[Wormwood]] cannot heal from eating food, the Tent allows him to deal with this restriction by converting Hunger points to Health (and Sanity). Assuming there is an abundant supply of food, Wormwood can repeatedly replenish his health this way in order to farm valuable [[Living Log]]s.
* Wearing a [[Slurper]], [[Belt of Hunger]], [[Hibearnation Vest]] or [[Funcap]] or playing as [[Wolfgang]], [[Wes]], or [[Warly]] doesn't change the [[Hunger]] cost of sleeping in a Tent.
* The Tent is one of the few structures in the game that costs [[Silk]]. Having it prebuilt can provide some emergency silk when carrying a [[Hammer]] to save inventory space.

==[[File:Placeholder.png|32px|link=]] Trivia==
* The Tent's original use was to allow the player to change their character. Crafting the tent cost more resources (15 Silk, 6 Twigs, and 3 Ropes), its crafting description was "''Change your skin''", and the Tent would be destroyed after one use. This functionality was changed in the 2013 [[Don't Starve/Version History#January 29, 2013 – Progress!|''Progress!'' update]].

==[[File:Mosquito.png|32px|link=]] Bugs==
* Sometimes after a 100% torch is equipped before sleeping, after the character sleeps the torch will still have a durability of 20%, and it will not lose durability. Unequipping and equipping the torch again will fix the bug.
* Sleeping while taking damage from [[Freezing]] may cause the edges of the screen to be stuck red. This will persist until the game is reloaded or if the character starts taking freezing damage again and warms back up.
* Sometimes a tent may be used 7 times before disappearing.
